sections.heading: HTML Templates
sections.body: The template element holds HTML content that isn't rendered when the page loads. It's used as a JavaScript template for creating dynamic content.

sections.heading: Meta Tags
sections.body:
Meta tags provide metadata about the HTML document. They go in the head element and control:
• Character encoding
• Viewport settings for responsive design
• Page description for SEO
• Social media sharing
• Theme color

sections.heading: Open Graph & Social Media
sections.body:
Open Graph meta tags control how your page appears when shared on social media:
• og:title - Page title
• og:description - Page description
• og:image - Preview image
• og:url - Page URL

jargon.definition: An HTML element that holds content not rendered until activated by JavaScript
HTML tags that provide metadata about the document
Protocol for controlling how content appears on social media platforms

summary: HTML templates hold dormant content for JavaScript. Meta tags control character encoding, viewport, SEO, and social media sharing.
